titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Bena Frank Mayer Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Chris Cole and Adrienne Pruitt

publicationstmt.publisher: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS).

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday April 21, 2025 at 19:00

corpname.part: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

did.abstract: The Bena Frank Mayer papers document Mayer's life as an artist, art teacher, and cofounder, with her husband Ralph, of the Artists Technical Research Institute. Her papers contain correspondence; biographical materials; records of artworks including photographic documentation and lists of works; sketches and paintings; diaries; publications; and auction and exhibition catalogs. Formats includes scrapbooks; framed photographs with glass, cased photographs and leather locket photographs, slides, negatives and transparencies; artwork including charcoal and pencil sketches and watercolors; and etching plates. Materials date from circa 1880s to 1991. Subjects include the Frank family history in Wyoming; Frank's artistic and familial networks; her artistic career, including exhibition history and examples of her work; and her working partnership with her husband Ralph in researching and providing information on technical aspects of art materials and methods.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Bena (Virginia) Frank Mayer was born on May 31, 1898, in Norfolk, Virginia, the daughter of Matilda Metzger Frank and Isaac Frank, and younger sister of Malcolm Gluck Frank (1891-1918). In the first few years of Mayer's life her family moved from Virginia to Newcastle, Wyoming where her father attempted to establish himself as a bank president. Following Mayer's childhood in Wyoming, her family moved back to Virginia before relocating to New York City in 1914.

In New York, Mayer enrolled in the Cooper Union and the Art Students League to pursue her visual art education before privately studying with Gifford Beal, Stuart Davis, George Luks, and Cecelia Beaux, in Paris. Mayer supported herself as a portrait painter throughout the 1920s, using the money she earned to finance an extended study trip to France, Spain and the Balearic Islands. Her work received many awards, including silver and bronze medals while a student at Cooper Union, first watercolor prize from the National Association of Women Artists (1951), the Marcia Tucker Award, and the Lena Newcastle Memorial Prize (1959), among others. She was also the recipient of numerous artist residencies including the Yaddo Fellow award (1956), the Huntington Hartford Foundation award (1957), the MacDowell Colony (1960 and 1966), as well as the Beaux Arts resident award at Hydra, Greece (1964). Her work was shown in the Carnegie International exhibition, the Whitney Museum of American Art, the University of Bangor (Maine), the Norfolk Museum of Art. She had solo shows at Simon's Rock in Great Barrington (1972) and the Yale School of Art (1990).

She met materials technologist and artist Ralph Mayer at a small social event hosted for young people at Temple Imanuel in New York. The two were married on July 25, 1927 in Manhattan. Together, Ralph and Bena Frank Mayer researched and edited several artist's reference books, including The Artists' Handbook of Materials and Techniques, first published in 1940. Their work together would eventually lead to the founding of the Artists Technical Research Institute (ATRI) in 1959, with the support of businessman and philanthropist Huntington Hartford. In addition to serving as co-founder and vice president of ATRI, Mayer also served as a board member for the National Association of Women Artists, the American Society of Contemporary Artists, and was twice president of the New York Society of Women Artists.

In 1990, Bena Frank Mayer established the Ralph Mayer Learning Center at the Yale University School of Art to house materials related to the Artists Technical Research Institute and support the continued study of artists' materials and techniques in the fields of painting and drawing. Steven Sheehan, the first director of the Mayer Center, edited and revised The Artists' Handbook for its 1991 edition. Mayer died in New York on August 22, 1991.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into six series: I. Artwork, 1914-circa 1991. II. Biographical Materials, 1887-1990. III. Correspondence, 1904-1991. IV. Photographs, circa 1880s-1990. V. Professional Papers, circa 1930s-1991. VI. Publications, 1927-1991.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Bena Frank Mayer papers, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Bena Frank Mayer papers document her personal and professional life and family history. Materials date from the 1880s until her death in 1991, and detail her artistic career, professional affiliations, and role in the establishment of the Artists Technical Research Institute (ATRI) with her husband Ralph Mayer, as well as her stewardship of his work following his death in 1979.

The collection consists of original works of art, sketches, studies, and reference photographs, as well as extensive diaries and autobiographical writings detailing her travels in Europe, her thoughts on marriage and domesticity, and her psychoanalysis. The collection also includes correspondence, photographs, and other documents related to Bena Frank Mayer's immediate and extended family, as well as that of her late husband, providing a fuller biographical portrait of her upbringing between Wyoming and Virginia. There are also numerous examples of work and written reflections on her time as an art student in New York in the 1910s to the 1920s. Membership lists, group exhibition catalogs and brochures document her involvement with many artistic organizations in New York.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: At the time of her death, Bena Frank had been organizing biographical materials in preparation of a memoir, and the Memoir materials subseries retains this received order. The remainder of the collections preserves the loose groupings of materials found at the time of receipt, but not their exact order. Titles provided by the creator are noted in quotation marks.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into two subseries: Bena Frank Mayer artwork and Ralph Mayer artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains original artworks, including drawings, sketches, watercolors, pastels, studies, as well as some reproductions of oil paintings produced by both Bena Frank and Ralph Mayer. Subjects represented in the collection include: urban scenes in and around New York City, landscapes, portraits (both commissioned and of the artist's friends and relatives), and cats and other animals. Much of the works present are in the form of sketchbooks, some of which document Bena Frank's travels in Europe between the 1940s and the early 1980s. The collection also contains some early examples of Bena Frank's artistic explorations and development from the 1920s shortly after completing her formal art education and before her marriage to Ralph Mayer.

The portion of this series pertaining specifically to Ralph Mayer contains numerous portfolios of drawings and studies, as well as color notes, reference photographs, sketchbooks, and some exhibition loan documents for Ralph Mayer's paintings. Examples of Ralph Mayer's work in the collection similarly represent subjects including urban scenes and landscapes in and around New York, as well as some produced during European travels between the 1950s and 1960s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing artistic process notes and biographical notes.

did.unittitle: "10 St. and AVB - New York City" mixed media drawing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes seven small plates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketches from life at Columbia University.

processinfo.p: Previously housed behind photograph of Bena Frank Mayer in France (Box 20, Folder 2).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials appear to have been Ralph Mayer's.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alternately spelled "Granada" on cover.

did.unittitle: Reproductions of paintings by Bena Frank
Sketches and reference photographs of Jim Hanson
"Impressions of London" sketchbook and travel diary
"1969" portfolio of sketches, photographs, and paintings
"3rd Ave and 20th St." sketches with reference photos
Portfolio of sketches, notes, and photographs
"Saratoga" portfolio of sketches, photographs, and newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some travel and technical notes.

did.unittitle: "Orchard St. Garment Market" photos and sketches

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into three subseries: General biographical materials, Family papers, and memoir materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The majority of this series is composed of Bena Frank Mayer's writing in the forms of poetry and prose, diaries (including reflections on her experience of psychoanalysis), travelogues, unpublished memoir drafts, notes on art, and other unpublished writings gathering her thoughts on feminism, marriage, and homemaking. Additionally present are diaries, wills, and news clippings related to other members of Bena Frank and Ralph Mayer's families, as well as miscellaneous personal documents such as Bena Frank's wedding certificate and anniversary materials. Family photographs may be found in Series IV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, sketches, and photographs.

did.unittitle: "The Sunbonnet Babies" illustrated book and letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Envelope of materials mostly pertaining to Ralph Mayer and Artists Technical Research Institute (ATRI).

scopecontent.p: Includes sketches, family history, art sale catalogs, and IIA notes.

did.unittitle: Ron Pisano correspondence to Bena Frank
National Association of Women Artists materials
Long Island Landscapes exhibition press clippings
Artists Technical Research Institute (ATRI) materials and auction catalog
N.Y. Society of Women Artists materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Membership lists and annual exhibition publications.

did.unittitle: Bena Frank and Ralph Mayer biographical materials
Bena Frank wills and biographical note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains letters and notes between Bena Frank and Ralph Mayer, their acquaintances, and family members dating from the 1900s to 1991. Among the items included are a letter from Ralph Mayer to Bena Frank written in morse code, a postcard from American artist Adolf Dehn, holiday and birthday cards, and an audio cassette of Ralph Mayer dictating responses to letters he had received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials found bound together with a ribbon; no organizing principle has been identified.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Uncle Nathan, Uncle Ben, Aunt Betty, Aunt Cora, Malcolm Gluck Frank, Matilda Metzger, Mother Mayer, Aunt Edna, and Pop.

did.unittitle: Birthday and holiday cards to Bena Frank
Birthday and holiday cards to Ralph Mayer
Bena Frank notes to Ralph Mayer
Ralph Mayer letter in Morse code
"Recording of Ralph Mayer dictating answers to letters he received"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Audio cassette tape

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains various printed, mounted, and/or framed photographs, primarily of Bena Frank and Ralph Mayer. Also included are early family photographs and albums, leather lockets, and photographs of friends and associates, such as Huntington Hartford. Materials date from the 1880s to the 1980s, and also include some snapshot photography by the Mayers of New York, Palm Beach, and various other locations.

did.unittitle: Unidentified Ralph Mayer framed family photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 5 framed photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two small sketches.

scopecontent.p: Photographs of Bena Frank and Ralph Mayer.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Previously housed in frame with watercolor sketch (Box 4, Folder 10).

did.unittitle: Ralph Mayer in his studio, mounted photograph
Photographs of people other than Bena Frank and Ralph Mayer
Bena Frank and Martin Bressler photos
Key West and wedding photographs and slides

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes materials related to Bena Frank's solo artistic career as well as her professional involvement with Ralph Mayer through the Artists Technical Research Institute (ATRI), which they co-founded in 1959. Also included are an early draft of Bena Frank's articulation of the impetus for founding ATRI, some personal correspondence of Ralph Mayer related to the use of painting varnishes, various drafts of Bena Frank's resume, loan and consignment documents, and materials related to solo exhibitions of Bena Frank's work.

did.unittitle: "Hilton's first letters and my first attempt at 'Revolution'"
Minutes of The Artists Technical Research Institute Inc.
Bena Frank loan and consignment documents
Bena Frank New York Artists Equity Association, Inc. certificate
New York Society of Women Artists membership list

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists mostly of exhibition catalogs and brochures for shows held by organizations of which Bena Frank and/or Ralph Mayer were members, such as the Allied Artists of America, the National Association of Women Artists, the American Society of Contemporary Artists, and the Artists Equity Association of New York. Additionally included are materials for a Works Progress Administration (WPA) group exhibition, the catalog of an auction to benefit the Israeli Soldiers Rest Camp, and the catalog of an exhibition focused on "The Eight," artists who taught at the Art Students League of New York and with whom Bena Frank Mayer studied.

did.unittitle: The Allied Artists of America Inc. Fourteenth Annual Exhibition catalog
National Association of Women Artists annual exhibitions materials
National Association of Women Artists annual exhibitions materials

The Immortal Eight and Its Influence exhibition catalog

scopecontent.p: Catalog for exhibition at The Art Students League of New York to which Bena Frank lent artwork.
